volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 469 - Section 2.546c)

sections.label: Section 469
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν ἱερὰς ἐξ ἀειρύτου χοὰς
sections.translation: First , from an ever-flowing

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: κρήνης ἐνεγκοῦ , διʼ ὁσίων χειρῶν θιγών .
sections.translation: spring bring sacred drink-offerings , borne in ritually pure hands .

sections.label: Section 471
sections.original: ὅταν δὲ τοῦτο χεῦμʼ ἀκήρατον λάβω ;
sections.translation: And when I have gotten this unmixed draught ?

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: κρατῆρές εἰσιν , ἀνδρὸς εὔχειρος τέχνη ,
sections.translation: There are bowls , the work of a skilled craftsman ; crown their edges and the handles at either side .

sections.label: Section 474
sections.original: θαλλοῖσιν ἢ κρόκαισιν , ἢ ποίῳ τρόπῳ ;
sections.translation: With olive branches , or woollen cloths , or in what way ?

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: οἰός γε νεαρᾶς νεοπόκῳ μαλλῷ λαβών .
sections.translation: Take the freshly-shorn wool of a ewe-lamb .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: εἶεν · τὸ δʼ ἔνθεν ποῖ τελευτῆσαί με χρή ;
sections.translation: Good ; and then to what last rite shall I proceed ?

sections.label: Section 477
sections.original: χοὰς χέασθαι στάντα πρὸς πρώτην ἕω .
sections.translation: Pour the drink-offerings , with your face to the dawn .

sections.label: Section 478
sections.original: ἦ τοῖσδε κρωσσοῖς οἷς λέγεις χέω τάδε ;
sections.translation: Shall I pour them with these vessels of which you speak ?

sections.label: Section 479
sections.original: τρισσάς γε πηγάς · τὸν τελευταῖον δʼ ὅλον .
sections.translation: Yes , in three streams ; but the last vessel —

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: τοῦ τόνδε πλήσας θῶ ; δίδασκε καὶ τόδε .
sections.translation: With what shall I fill this , before I set it down ? Teach me this also .

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: ὕδατος , μελίσσης · μηδὲ προσφέρειν μέθυ .
sections.translation: With water and honey ; but add no wine .

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: ὅταν δὲ τούτων γῆ μελάμφυλλος τύχῃ ;
sections.translation: And when the ground under the dark shade has drunk these ?

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: τρὶς ἐννέʼ αὐτῇ κλῶνας ἐξ ἀμφοῖν χεροῖν
sections.translation: Three times lay on it nine branches of olive with both your hands , and meanwhile make this prayer .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: τούτων ἀκοῦσαι βούλομαι · μέγιστα γάρ .
sections.translation: I wish to hear this prayer ; it is the most important part .

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: ὥς σφας καλοῦμεν Εὐμενίδας , ἐξ εὐμενῶν
sections.translation: We call them Eumenides , so that with well-wishing power they may receive the suppliant as his saviors . Let this be your prayer , or of whoever prays for you . Speak inaudibly , and do not lift up your voice ; then depart , without looking behind .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἔπειτʼ ἀφέρπειν ἄστροφος . καὶ ταῦτά σοι
sections.translation: If you should do this , I would be bold enough to come to your aid ; but otherwise , stranger , I would fear for you .

sections.label: Section 493
sections.original: ὦ παῖδε , κλύετον τῶνδε προσχώρων ξένων ;
sections.translation: Daughters , do you hear these strangers who dwell nearby ?

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: ἠκούσαμέν τε χὤ τι δεῖ πρόστασσε δρᾶν .
sections.translation: We have listened . Tell us what to do .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν οὐχ ὁδωτά · λείπομαι γὰρ ἐν
sections.translation: I cannot make the trip ; for I am disabled by lack of strength and lack of sight , twin evils . But let one of you two go and do these things . For I think that one soul suffices to pay this debt for ten thousand , if it comes with good will .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐν τάχει τι πράσσετον · μόνον δέ με
sections.translation: Act , then , with speed . But do not abandon me , for my body would not have the strength to move , without help or a guiding hand .

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶμʼ ἐγὼ τελοῦσα · τὸν τόπον δʼ ἵνα
sections.translation: Then I will go to perform the rite ; but where I am to find the place — this I wish to learn .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: τοὐκεῖθεν ἄλσους , ὦ ξένη , τοῦδʼ · ἢν δέ του
sections.translation: On the further side of this grove , stranger . And if you have need of anything , there is a guardian of the place . He will direct you .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: χωροῖμʼ ἂν ἐς τόδʼ · Ἀντιγόνη , σὺ δʼ ἐνθάδε
sections.translation: Off to my task . But you , Antigone , watch our father here . In the case of parents , if we toil , we must not keep a memory of it . Ismene exits .

sections.label: Section 1.510
sections.original: δεινὸν μὲν τὸ πάλαι κείμενον ἤδη κακόν , ὦ ξεῖνʼ , ἐπεγείρειν ·
sections.translation: Terrible it is , stranger , to arouse the old woe that has for so long been laid to rest : and yet I yearn to hear —

sections.label: Section 1.512
sections.original: τί τοῦτο ;
sections.translation: What now ?

sections.label: Section 1.513
sections.original: τᾶς δειλαίας ἀπόρου φανείσας
sections.translation: — Of that grief-filled anguish , cureless , with which you have wrestled .

sections.label: Section 1.515
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς ξενίας ἀνοίξῃς
sections.translation: By your hospitality , do not uncover the shame that I have suffered !

sections.label: Section 1.517
sections.original: τό τοι πολὺ καὶ μηδαμὰ λῆγον
sections.translation: Seeing that the tale is wide-spread and in no way weakens , I wish , friend , to hear it straight .

sections.label: Section 1.519
sections.original: ὤμοι .
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1.519a
sections.original: στέρξον , ἱκετεύω .
sections.translation: Grant the favor , I beg !

sections.label: Section 1.519b
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ .
sections.translation: Alas , alas !

sections.label: Section 1.520
sections.original: πείθου · κἀγὼ γὰρ ὅσον σὺ προσχρῄζεις .
sections.translation: Grant my wish , as I have granted yours to the full .

sections.label: Section 1.521
sections.original: ἤνεγκʼ οὖν κακότατʼ , ὦ ξένοι , ἤνεγκʼ ἀέκων μέν ,
sections.translation: I have suffered the greatest misery , strangers — suffered it through unintended deeds — may the god know it ! No part was of my own choice .

sections.label: Section 1.524
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐς τί ;
sections.translation: But in what way ?

sections.label: Section 1.525
sections.original: κακᾷ μʼ εὐνᾷ πόλις οὐδὲν ἴδριν
sections.translation: In an evil marriage , the city bound me , unknowing , to ruin .

sections.label: Section 1.527
sections.original: ἦ ματρόθεν , ὡς ἀκούω ,
sections.translation: Is it true , as I hear , that you made your mother the partner of your bed , to its infamy ?

sections.label: Section 1.529
sections.original: ὤμοι θάνατος μὲν τάδʼ ἀκούειν ,
sections.translation: Ah , me ! These words , strangers , are like death to my ears . And those two maidens of mine —

sections.label: Section 1.530
sections.original: ὦ ξεῖνʼ · αὗται δὲ δύʼ ἐξ ἐμοῦ μὲν
sections.translation: What will you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.531
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ;
sections.translation: O Zeus !

sections.label: Section 1.532
sections.original: παῖδε , δύο δʼ ἄτα
sections.translation: — of mine , sprung from the travail of the womb that bore me too .

sections.label: Section 1.534
sections.original: ματρὸς κοινᾶς ἀπέβλαστον ὠδῖνος .
sections.translation: These , then , are at once your daughters , and —

sections.label: Section 2.535
sections.original: σαί τʼ εἴσʼ ἄρʼ ἀπόγονοί τε καὶ
sections.translation: — Sisters , indeed , of their father .

sections.label: Section 2.536
sections.original: κοιναί γε πατρὸς ἀδελφεαί .
sections.translation: Oh !

sections.label: Section 2.537
sections.original: ἰώ .
sections.translation: You have suffered —

sections.label: Section 2.537a
sections.original: ἰὼ δῆτα μυρίων γʼ ἐπιστροφαὶ κακῶν .
sections.translation: I have suffered woes grievous to bear .

sections.label: Section 2.538
sections.original: ἔπαθες
sections.translation: You have performed —

sections.label: Section 2.538a
sections.original: ἔπαθον ἄλαστʼ ἔχειν .
sections.translation: I have not performed !

sections.label: Section 2.539
sections.original: ἔρεξας
sections.translation: How ?

sections.label: Section 2.539a
sections.original: οὐκ ἔρεξα .
sections.translation: A gift was given to me — O , wretched that I am , if only I had never won from the city that gift for my services !

sections.label: Section 2.542
sections.original: δύστανε , τί γάρ ; ἔθου φόνον
sections.translation: Cursed man ! What of this ? Did you commit the murder —

sections.label: Section 2.543
sections.original: τί τοῦτο ; τί δʼ ἐθέλεις μαθεῖν ;
sections.translation: What now ? What would you learn ?

sections.label: Section 2.544
sections.original: πατρός ;
sections.translation: — Of your father ?

sections.label: Section 2.544a
sections.original: παπαῖ . δευτέραν ἔπαισας , ἐπὶ νόσῳ νόσον ,
sections.translation: Oh ! oh ! a second stab — wound on wound !

sections.label: Section 2.545
sections.original: ἔκανες
sections.translation: You killed —

sections.label: Section 2.545a
sections.original: ἔκανον . ἔχει δέ μοι
sections.translation: I killed — yet I have a plea —

sections.label: Section 2.546
sections.original: τί τοῦτο ;
sections.translation: What can you plead ?

sections.label: Section 2.546a
sections.original: πρὸς δίκας τι .
sections.translation: — A plea of justice .

sections.label: Section 2.546b
sections.original: τί γάρ ;
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 2.546c
sections.original: ἐγὼ φράσω .
sections.translation: I will tell you : I killed in ignorance and perished utterly . Pure before the law , without knowledge of my act , I have come to this !
